evidence: R2 running-config -> access_lists -> name: "R2_IN" contains ace-rule sequence 10: { "action": "deny", "protocol": "ip", "dst-host": "224.0.0.5" }
R2 running-config -> access_lists -> name: "R2_IN" contains ace-rule sequence 20: { "action": "deny", "protocol": "ip", "dst-host": "224.0.0.6" }
R2 get_ospf -> ospf_full entry for interface_name: "Serial0/1/0" -> "neighbors": [] (no neighbor seen on that interface)

R1 get_ospf -> ospf_full entry for interface_name: "Serial0/1/0" shows neighbor_id: "2.2.2.2" neighbor_address: "10.0.0.2" neighbor_state: "ospf-nbr-init" (R1 receives R2 Hellos but adjacency is stuck at INIT)
Interface status: both R1 Serial0/1/0 and R2 Serial0/1/0 show admin_state: "up", oper_state: "up" (link is up, signaling problem rather than physical)

plan_history.intent_description: One-way OSPF Hello on the R1<->R2 Serial0/1/0 link: R1 sees R2 (INIT) but R2 reports no neighbor. Restore OSPF adjacency so the neighbor becomes UP and routes are exchanged.
plan_history.problem: One-way OSPF Hello reception on R2 Serial0/1/0 causing adjacency to remain down and preventing route exchange between R1 and R2.
plan_history.fix_summary: On router2, configure Serial0/1/0 with the expected IP (10.0.0.2/30), ensure the interface is administratively up, and enable OSPF on the interface (OSPF process 1, area 0) so Hellos are transmitted/received and the adjacency can form.

plan_history.intent_description: One-way OSPF Hello on the R1<->R2 Serial0/1/0 link: R1 sees R2 (INIT) but R2 reports no neighbor. Restore OSPF adjacency so the neighbor becomes UP and routes are exchanged.
plan_history.problem: Inbound ACL R2_IN on router2 Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF Hellos and forming adjacency with R1.
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the deny ACEs for OSPF multicast from ACL R2_IN applied inbound on Serial0/1/0 of router2 so OSPF Hellos are allowed and adjacency can form.

remaining_issues: Running config still shows access-group in on Serial0/1/0 referencing ACL "R2_IN" while the access_lists section does not contain a definition for "R2_IN". This is a configuration inconsistency that should be cleaned up or verified (either remove the applied access-group or ensure the intended ACL is present and correct).
